channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah exchanged strikes in Lebanon as US-Iran talks stalled. Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi continued diplomatic efforts over the weekend, but US President Donald Trump scrapped a visit to Pakistan by his envoys, casting doubt on US-Iran peace hopes. The stalemate risks escalating tensions without a deal.

item.title: US-Iran peace hopes fade despite foreign minister's diplomatic push
item.description: US President Donald Trump cancelled a visit to Pakistan by his envoys, undermining Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's diplomatic push for peace. The move suggests fading hopes for a US-Iran agreement, despite Araghchi's continued shuttle diplomacy.

item.title: First trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
item.description: A Damascus court is trying former Syrian officials, including former President Bashar Assad and his brother, in absentia. Assad is believed to have fled to Russia, while one official is being tried in person. The trial marks a rare step towards accountability.

item.title: Pogacar outlasts debutant Seixas to claim third straight Liege title
item.description: Slovenia's Tadej Pogacar won his third consecutive Liege-Bastogne-Liege cycling title, outlasting debutant Joao Almeida Seixas. Pogacar's victory solidified his position as a top contender in the cycling world. Pogacar outlasts debutant Seixas to claim third straight Liege title.

item.title: Mohammad-Bagher Ghalibaf
item.description: a veteran of the Iran-Iraq war and former IRGC commander, emerged as the new face of Iran's revolutionary system. Ghalibaf's rise comes as the country faces external threats and internal pressure, with much of its top leadership killed or displaced.

item.title: Mali Defence Minister Killed In Bomb Attack As Army vs Separatists War Rages
item.description: Mali's Defence Minister was killed in a bomb attack as fighting resumed in Kita and other areas. Fighting resumed Sunday in several areas, including Kita near Bamako, Kidal, Gao and Severe.

item.title: Setback For Netanyahu In Corruption Case, Israel President Wants Plea Deal
item.description: Israel's President wants a plea deal for Netanyahu, who denies bribery and fraud charges. Netanyahu denies the bribery, fraud and breach of trust charges. (File). Setback For Netanyahu In Corruption Case, Israel President Wants Plea Deal.

item.title: Iran FM back in Pakistan after Oman visit, says report
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi discussed security in the Strait of Hormuz with Oman. Iran's Foreign ​Minister Abbas Araghchi ‌held talks in ​Oman, discussed security ‌in the Strait of Hormuz, ‌broader ⁠Gulf waters, ⁠and diplomatic efforts to end the ​Iran-U. S.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran shares 'workable framework' with Pak on ending
item.description: Iran shared a framework to end the war with Pakistan as the US holds back its envoy. US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: Iran shares 'workable framework' with Pak on ending war as US holds back envoy for talks The Times of India.

item.title: UNRWA schools in West Bank drop to four-day weeks over funding issues
item.description: UNRWA schools in the West Bank reduced to four-day weeks due to funding issues. UNRWA schools in West Bank drop to four-day weeks over funding issues The Jerusalem Post.

item.title: Iran president calls on US to end blockade. Follow live updates.
item.description: Iran's president called on the US to end its blockade. Iran president calls on US to end blockade. Follow live updates. The Boston Globe.

item.title: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine marked 40 years since the world's worst nuclear disaster. 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine.

item.title: Israel feared Amal Khalil, just as it did Shireen Abu Akleh
item.description: Amal Khalil was killed for refusing to be silenced. Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed because she refused to be intimidated into silence. Israel feared Amal Khalil, just as it did Shireen Abu Akleh.

item.title: Mali defence minister killed after coordinated attacks
item.description: Mali's Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks. Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara has been killed after coordinated attacks on military sites and his home. Mali defence minister killed after coordinated attacks.

item.title: ‘Man with serious weapon started shooting suddenly’: Eyewitnesses recount chilling moments from
item.description: An eyewitness described a man suddenly shooting with a serious weapon. ‘Man with serious weapon started shooting suddenly’: Eyewitnesses recount chilling moments from.

item.title: Trump WH correspondents' dinner shooting LIVE: Suspect believed to have been targeting
item.description: A suspect targeted the White House correspondents' dinner in Washington. Acting U. S. Attorney General Todd Blanche said officials believe that the suspect traveled by train to Chicago and then on to Washington, where he checked in as a guest to the hotel where one of Washington's glitziest events was being held Saturday night.

item.title: Pakistan eases restrictions on twin cities with no possibility of U.S.-Iran talks
item.description: Pakistan eased restrictions on twin cities due to no US-Iran talks. The deployment of police and other law enforcement personnel remains active, but restrictions on the movement of regular commuters have been relaxed, according to police.

item.title: Gunman believed to be targeting Trump administration officials: U.S. Attorney General
item.description: US Attorney General says a gunman targeted Trump administration officials. The suspect will be charged with assault and discharging a firearm on April 27, 2026. The suspect will be formally charged in federal court on Monday (April 27, 2026) with assault ‌of a federal officer ​and discharging a firearm in an attempt to kill a ⁠federal officer.

item.title: Has China Teased Its 4th Aircraft Carrier? 'He Jian' In Video Sparks Buzz
item.description: China may have revealed its 4th aircraft carrier in a video. A model of the Fujian Type 003 aircraft carrier is on display. Visitors look at a model of the Fujian Type 003 aircraft carrier (File).
